rationale: Both candidates correctly verified the runner fix's root cause and the streaming FINAL_ONLY scoping (matching the reviewer's main validated points), and both independently flagged the same async_chunk default=True inconsistency that echoes the ground truth's Low-severity robustness concern. X edges ahead because its note that the split 'keys solely on total_num_scheduled_tokens' and drops hidden-shape alignment directly anticipates the single-vs-multi-axis gap later fixed by PR #4910, while Y's analogous NPU-parity finding, though grounded, doesn't touch the batch-axis issue. Neither candidate addressed the amy-why-3459 'move variable into model' design question.
